 {"id":"bd-2hd0","title":"Tests: /resume /new /tree /fork session UX","description":"# Goal
Add automated coverage for the interactive session UX parity work.

# Scope
- In-app `/resume` selection loads correct session.
- Session picker delete flow.
- `/new` creates a new session without clobbering prior.
- `/tree` navigation + summary entry logic.
- `/fork` creates and switches to new session file.
- Double-escape behavior.

# Testing Strategy
- Prefer TUI state tests (`tests/tui_state.rs`) for key flows.
- Use temp dirs for session storage.
- Mock `trash` and other external commands via injectable command runner.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests cover the major user-facing flows and prevent regressions.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-2hr","title":"Spec: Unified JS+WASM capability model","description":"# Goal
Define a **single, coherent capability model** that applies equally to JS (PiJS) and WASM extensions, so security policy, logs, and tooling are identical across runtimes.

# Why
- Avoid divergent permission semantics between JS and WASM.
- Enable shared tooling (manifests, scanners, audit logs) and a consistent user mental model.
- JS-tier may execute wasm via PiWasm bridge (bd-1ry); capability semantics must remain identical.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Capability taxonomy: common names, scopes, and semantics across JS/WASM.
- Hostcall mapping: how JS `pi.*` calls and WASM hostcalls map to the same capabilities.
- Manifest alignment: how a single manifest expresses capabilities for both runtimes.
- Logging schema alignment: identical fields and redaction rules.

# Constraints
- Must compose with `bd-37z` ABI.
- Must be compatible with WASM host (`bd-nom`) and JS runtime (`bd-123`).

# Tests This Enables
- Cross-runtime capability conformance tests.
- E2E security suite verifying identical enforcement paths.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:09:44.048778878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:25:20.016597430Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:22:35.300367473Z","close_reason":"Unified JS+WASM capability model spec added","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hr","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hr","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2hz","title":"Extension UI protocol integration (interactive + RPC)","description":"# Background
- Legacy spec defines `extension_ui_request` / `extension_ui_response` covering: `confirm`, `select`, `input`, `editor`, plus fire-and-forget notifications.
- `src/extensions.rs` already has:
  - `ExtensionUiRequest` + `ExtensionUiResponse` types
  - `ExtensionManager::{request_ui, respond_ui}` with timeout support
- `src/interactive.rs` already has an extension UI queue and an interactive handler (`handle_extension_ui_request`).

# Gap (Observed)
- RPC mode is not end-to-end:
  - `src/rpc.rs` accepts a command `extension_ui_response` but **does not route** the response to the extension runtime.
  - RPC mode **never emits** `extension_ui_request` events, so headless controllers cannot respond.
- Runtime dispatch is not guaranteed to be expressed via the unified Hostcall ABI taxonomy/logging. The UI surface must converge on the `host_call` / `host_result` contract (bd-1uy.1.*), not grow its own ad-hoc error mapping.

# Goal
Make extension UI requests work end-to-end in **both**:
- interactive TUI mode
- RPC mode (stdin/stdout protocol)

and ensure the behavior is:
- deterministic (queueing + ordering)
- capability/policy compatible
- taxonomy-correct (`timeout|denied|io|invalid_request|internal`)
- observable (structured logs, redaction rules)

# Scope / Deliverables
1) **Contract + normalization**
- Enumerate the supported UI methods and their payload shapes (defaults, required fields, cancel semantics).
- Define response normalization rules so JS + WASM + protocol adapter behave identically.

2) **Interactive integration**
- Ensure each UI method renders with clear provenance:
  - show extension id/name
  - show requested method
  - show timeout/cancel affordance
- Ensure queue semantics are sane:
  - one active request at a time
  - bounded queue
  - cancellation does not deadlock

3) **RPC integration**
- Emit `extension_ui_request` events on stdout.
- Accept `extension_ui_response` commands on stdin.
- Enforce request/response id matching, timeouts, and cancellation.

4) **Conformance + E2E**
- Add conformance fixtures for each method + edge cases (timeout, cancel, invalid payloads).
- Add E2E scenarios that exercise UI via:
  - interactive tmux capture
  - RPC controller script
- All scenarios emit detailed JSONL logs + deterministic artifact indexes per bd-4u9.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A sample extension can invoke confirm/select/input/editor and receive responses in both interactive + RPC modes.
- [ ] RPC emits `extension_ui_request` and routes `extension_ui_response` back into the runtime.
- [ ] Timeouts and cancellation propagate correctly without deadlocks.
- [ ] Error mapping uses only hostcall taxonomy codes.
- [ ] Fixtures pass and logs are deterministic.
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.808698612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:32:26.898542639Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","hostcall","interactive","rpc","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-2i5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-576","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2hz.1","title":"Spec: Extension UI API contract (methods, payloads, defaults, errors)","description":"# Goal
Define the *exact* extension UI contract, spec-first, so both interactive + RPC implementations can be correct and deterministic.

# Background
- The runtime uses `ExtensionUiRequest` / `ExtensionUiResponse` (host <-> UI surface).
- Legacy extensions expect stable method names + payload shapes.
- If we get payload defaults/cancel semantics wrong, extensions will subtly break.

# Scope
- Enumerate supported `method` values (minimum):
  - `confirm`
  - `select`
  - `input`
  - `editor`
  - `notify` (fire-and-forget)
- For each method, define:
  - required fields
  - optional fields + default values
  - validation rules and `invalid_request` mapping
  - response shape (`value` vs `cancelled`)
  - timeout semantics (`payload.timeout` vs hostcall timeout vs global budget)
  - cancellation semantics (user cancel, timeout cancel, shutdown)
  - deterministic redaction rules (never log raw secrets)

# Deliverables
- A written spec in this bead that is detailed enough to implement without re-reading legacy markdown.
- A mapping table:
  - `ExtensionUiRequest` <-> RPC `extension_ui_request` event shape
  - RPC `extension_ui_response` command <-> `ExtensionUiResponse`

# Implementation Notes
- Prefer a single normalized payload per method so interactive and RPC share the same validation/normalization code.
- IDs:
  - `request.id` MUST be stable and round-tripped; it is the correlation key for queueing, logs, and timeouts.

# Tests
- Add unit tests for request validation + normalization once the contract is finalized (implementation lives in follow-up beads; this bead is spec-only).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Spec is complete enough to implement without consulting legacy docs
[ ] Every method has explicit required/optional fields + defaults + validation rules
[ ] Response shapes and cancellation semantics are fully specified
[ ] RPC mapping is explicitly defined and unambiguous
[ ] Deterministic logging + redaction rules are specified","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:34:53.343740921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:36:26.764797668Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","rpc","spec","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hz.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2hz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:34:53.343740921Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2hz.1","title":"Spec: Extension UI API contract (methods, payloads, defaults, errors)","description":"# Goal
Define the *exact* extension UI contract, spec-first, so both interactive + RPC implementations can be correct and deterministic.

# Background
- The runtime uses `ExtensionUiRequest` / `ExtensionUiResponse` (host <-> UI surface).
- Legacy extensions expect stable method names + payload shapes.
- If we get payload defaults/cancel semantics wrong, extensions will subtly break.

# Scope
- Enumerate supported `method` values (minimum):
  - `confirm`
  - `select`
  - `input`
  - `editor`
  - `notify` (fire-and-forget)
- For each method, define:
  - required fields
  - optional fields + default values
  - validation rules and `invalid_request` mapping
  - response shape (`value` vs `cancelled`)
  - timeout semantics (`payload.timeout` vs hostcall timeout vs global budget)
  - cancellation semantics (user cancel, timeout cancel, shutdown)
  - deterministic redaction rules (never log raw secrets)

# Deliverables
- A written spec in this bead that is detailed enough to implement without re-reading legacy markdown.
- A mapping table:
  - `ExtensionUiRequest` <-> RPC `extension_ui_request` event shape
  - RPC `extension_ui_response` command <-> `ExtensionUiResponse`

# Implementation Notes
- Prefer a single normalized payload per method so interactive and RPC share the same validation/normalization code.
- IDs:
  - `request.id` MUST be stable and round-tripped; it is the correlation key for queueing, logs, and timeouts.

# Tests
- Add unit tests for request validation + normalization once the contract is finalized (implementation lives in follow-up beads; this bead is spec-only).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Spec is complete enough to implement without consulting legacy docs
[ ] Every method has explicit required/optional fields + defaults + validation rules
[ ] Response shapes and cancellation semantics are fully specified
[ ] RPC mapping is explicitly defined and unambiguous
[ ] Deterministic logging + redaction rules are specified","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RosePrairie","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:34:53.343740921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:33:54.086995081Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T08:33:54.086968151Z","close_reason":"Spec complete (see comment)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","rpc","spec","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hz.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2hz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:34:53.343740921Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":528,"issue_id":"bd-2hz.1","author":"RosePrairie","text":"# Extension UI API Contract (v1)

This is the **spec-first** contract for the extension UI surface (`pi.ui.*` / `ctx.ui.*`) across:
- **Interactive TUI** mode
- **RPC** mode (stdin/stdout protocol)
- **Non-interactive** modes (print/headless without a UI surface)

Goal: make UI behavior **deterministic**, **compatible with pi-mono**, and implementable without re-reading legacy docs.

---

## 1) Principles

- **Deterministic defaults:** If a dialog is cancelled or times out, the extension receives a **default value** (no thrown error).
- **Two categories:**
  - **Dialog methods** block awaiting a response.
  - **Fire-and-forget methods** never block and never require a response.
- **Capability-gated:** UI hostcalls are gated by capability `ui` (policy may prompt/deny).
- **Redaction-first logging:** Never log raw user-entered values (input/editor) or editor text mutations.

---

## 2) Envelopes (Normative)

### 2.1 Host internal structs (Rust)

**`ExtensionUiRequest`** (host -> UI surface)
- `id: string` (required)
  - Correlation key.
  - MUST be stable and round-tripped.
- `method: string` (required)
- `payload: object` (required; non-object is `invalid_request` for supported methods)
- `timeout_ms: number|null` (optional)

**`ExtensionUiResponse`** (UI surface -> host)
- `id: string` (required)
- `cancelled: boolean` (required)
- `value: any|null` (optional)

### 2.2 RPC wire format (stdin/stdout)

**Request event (stdout):** `extension_ui_request`

Canonical shape:
```json
{ "type": "extension_ui_request", "id": "...", "method": "confirm", "title": "...", "message": "...", "timeout": 5000 }
```

Rules:
- Always include `type`, `id`, `method`.
- If `payload` is an object, it is **flattened into top-level fields**.
- If `payload` is non-object, include `payload: <any>`.

**Response command (stdin):** `extension_ui_response`

Canonical (recommended):
```json
{ "type": "extension_ui_response", "requestId": "...", "value": "..." }
{ "type": "extension_ui_response", "requestId": "...", "cancelled": true }
```

Compatibility inputs the host SHOULD accept:
- `requestId` alias: `id`
- For `confirm`: `confirmed: boolean` is treated as `value: boolean`

Mapping to `ExtensionUiResponse`:
- `response.id = requestId || id`
- `response.cancelled = cancelled == true`
- `response.value = value || confirmed` (if present)

---

## 3) Supported Methods (v1)

Dialog methods (expect response):
- `select`
- `confirm`
- `input`
- `editor`

Fire-and-forget methods:
- `notify`
- `setStatus` (alias: `set_status`)
- `setWidget` (alias: `set_widget`)
- `setTitle` (alias: `set_title`)
- `set_editor_text` (optional alias: `setEditorText`)

Unknown methods:
- v1 recommendation: treat as `invalid_request` (do not silently succeed).

---

## 4) Method Specs

### 4.1 `select`

**Purpose:** prompt the user to choose from options.

Payload fields:
- `title: string` (required)
- `message: string` (optional, default `""`)
- `options: array` (required)
  - Each item may be:
    - **string**: treated as `{ label: <string>, value: <string> }`
    - **object**:
      - `label: string` (required)
      - `value: any` (optional)
      - `description: string` (optional)
      - `detail: string` (optional)
- `timeout: number` (optional, ms)

Validation:
- `options` MUST be an array.
- Each option object MUST have non-empty `label`.

Response:
- `cancelled=false`: `value` is:
  - the chosen option’s `value` if present, otherwise the option’s `label` string.
- `cancelled=true` or timeout: host returns `null`.

RPC request example:
```json
{ "type": "extension_ui_request", "id": "uuid-1", "method": "select", "title": "Pick one", "options": ["A","B"], "timeout": 10000 }
```

RPC response examples:
```json
{ "type": "extension_ui_response", "requestId": "uuid-1", "value": "A" }
{ "type": "extension_ui_response", "requestId": "uuid-1", "cancelled": true }
```

---

### 4.2 `confirm`

**Purpose:** yes/no confirmation.

Payload fields:
- `title: string` (required)
- `message: string` (required; may be empty)
- `timeout: number` (optional, ms)

Notes:
- Internal capability prompts are also modeled as `confirm` with extra fields:
  - `capability: string`
  - `extension_id: string`
  - UI implementations MAY render these with a dedicated UX.
  - Unknown extra fields MUST be ignored.

Response:
- `cancelled=false`: boolean in `value`.
- `cancelled=true` or timeout: host returns **`false`**.

RPC request example:
```json
{ "type": "extension_ui_request", "id": "uuid-2", "method": "confirm", "title": "Clear?", "message": "This cannot be undone", "timeout": 5000 }
```

RPC response examples:
```json
{ "type": "extension_ui_response", "requestId": "uuid-2", "value": true }
{ "type": "extension_ui_response", "requestId": "uuid-2", "cancelled": true }
```

---

### 4.3 `input`

**Purpose:** single-line free-form text input.

Payload fields:
- `title: string` (required)
- `message: string` (optional)
- `placeholder: string` (optional)
- `default: string` (optional; prefill)
- `timeout: number` (optional, ms)

Response:
- `cancelled=false`: `value` is a string.
- `cancelled=true` or timeout: host returns `null`.

RPC request example:
```json
{ "type": "extension_ui_request", "id": "uuid-3", "method": "input", "title": "Name", "placeholder": "type..." }
```

---

### 4.4 `editor`

**Purpose:** multi-line text editor.

Payload fields:
- `title: string` (required)
- `message: string` (optional)
- `prefill: string` (optional; legacy field)
- `default: string` (optional; alias of `prefill`)
- `language: string` (optional; hint for syntax highlighting)
- `timeout: number` (optional, ms)

Normalization:
- If both `prefill` and `default` are present, `prefill` wins.

Response:
- `cancelled=false`: `value` is a string.
- `cancelled=true` or timeout: host returns `null`.

RPC request example:
```json
{ "type": "extension_ui_request", "id": "uuid-4", "method": "editor", "title": "Edit", "prefill": "Line 1\nLine 2" }
```

---

### 4.5 `notify`

**Purpose:** show a non-blocking notification.

Payload fields:
- `message: string` (required)
- `title: string` (optional)
- `notifyType: "info"|"warning"|"error"` (optional)
  - Aliases: `level`
  - Default: `"info"`

No response is expected.

RPC request example:
```json
{ "type": "extension_ui_request", "id": "uuid-5", "method": "notify", "message": "Done", "notifyType": "info" }
```

---

### 4.6 `setStatus` / `set_status`

**Purpose:** set or clear a keyed status entry.

Payload fields:
- `statusKey: string` (required)
  - Aliases: `status_key`
- `statusText: string|null` (optional)
  - Aliases: `status_text`, `text`

Semantics:
- If `statusText` is missing/null/empty => clear the status for that key.
- Otherwise set/update.

No response is expected.

---

### 4.7 `setWidget` / `set_widget`

**Purpose:** set or clear a widget (block of text lines).

Payload fields:
- `widgetKey: string` (required)
  - Aliases: `widget_key`
- `widgetLines: string[]|null` (optional)
  - Aliases: `widget_lines`, `lines`
- `widgetPlacement: "aboveEditor"|"belowEditor"` (optional)
  - Aliases: `widget_placement`, `placement`
  - Default: `"aboveEditor"`
- `content: string` (optional)
  - If present and `widgetLines` missing, host MAY derive `widgetLines = content.split("\n")`.

Semantics:
- If no lines/content => clear the widget for that key.

No response is expected.

---

### 4.8 `setTitle` / `set_title`

Payload fields:
- `title: string` (required)

No response is expected.

---

### 4.9 `set_editor_text` (alias: `setEditorText`)

Payload fields:
- `text: string` (required)

No response is expected.

---

## 5) Timeout + Cancellation Semantics

Inputs that can provide a timeout:
- `HostCallPayload.timeout_ms` (outer deadline for the hostcall)
- `ExtensionUiRequest.timeout_ms` (host-provided)
- `payload.timeout` (dialog-level auto-dismiss)

Effective deadline:
- `effective_timeout_ms = min(non-null timeouts)`.

On timeout:
- Dialog methods MUST behave like **cancellation** (return defaults), not a `timeout` error.

On user cancellation:
- UI surface sends `{cancelled:true}`; host resolves with defaults.

On shutdown / UI surface disappearance:
- Prefer cancellation defaults if possible; otherwise map to taxonomy `io`.

Default return values:
- `select`/`input`/`editor` => `null`
- `confirm` => `false`

---

## 6) Validation + Error Mapping (v1)

- `invalid_request`
  - missing required fields
  - wrong types
  - unknown `method` (recommended)
- `denied`
  - policy denies capability `ui` (or UI surface not permitted)
- `io`
  - UI surface channel closed while `hasUI=true`
- `internal`
  - invariant violations/bugs

---

## 7) Deterministic Logging + Redaction (Normative)

When logging UI requests/responses (extension logs, hostcall logs, RPC traces):

- Never log raw values for:
  - `input.value`, `editor.value`
  - `set_editor_text.text`
  - `editor.prefill/default`
- Log request metadata only:
  - `id`, `method`, `timeout_ms`, `title_len`, `message_len`, `options_count`, `widget_lines_count`
- Log response metadata only:
  - `id`, `method`, `cancelled`, `value_type`, `value_len` (string length only)

---

## 8) Current Rust Implementation Gaps (for follow-up beads)

Known mismatches to resolve in bd-2hz.2/.3/.4:
- `ExtensionManager::request_ui()` currently returns an **error** on timeout; v1 spec requires treating timeouts as **cancellation**.
- `confirm` cancellation currently resolves to `null` via hostcall output; v1 spec requires `false`.
- RPC command handler currently expects `requestId` and does not accept legacy `{id, confirmed}`; v1 spec recommends accepting those aliases.
","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:32:41Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2hz.2","title":"Impl: RPC mode extension UI bridging (emit request, accept response)","description":"# Goal
Make extension UI requests work end-to-end in **RPC mode**:
- host emits `extension_ui_request` events on stdout
- controller sends `extension_ui_response` commands on stdin
- runtime routes the response back to the correct pending request

# Background (Observed)
- `src/rpc.rs` currently accepts `extension_ui_response` but does not route it into the extension UI pending map.
- RPC mode currently never emits `extension_ui_request` events.

# Scope
- Implement the RPC-side UI surface as a proper bridge:
  - outbound: `ExtensionUiRequest::to_rpc_event()` -> send on RPC out channel
  - inbound: parse `extension_ui_response` -> `ExtensionUiResponse` -> route to pending request
- Enforce:
  - strict ID match (`id`)
  - method-specific response shape validation (per bd-2hz.1)
  - timeouts and cancellation semantics
  - deterministic ordering (queue: one active UI request at a time)

# Design Notes
- Avoid deadlocks:
  - response routing must not hold locks across await points.
  - do not block the main RPC read loop indefinitely; use oneshot + pending map.
- Error mapping:
  - any bridge failures map to hostcall taxonomy codes (via bd-1uy.1.1 once integrated)
  - do not introduce new ad-hoc codes.
- Logging:
  - emit `pi.ext.log.v1` UI events with correlation ids + timing
  - never log raw payload fields; log summary + redaction notes

# Tests
- Unit tests:
  - `extension_ui_request` JSON shape is stable
  - response routing by id
  - timeout returns taxonomy `timeout`
  - cancelled responses map correctly (`cancelled=true`)
  - invalid payloads -> `invalid_request`
- Integration test:

 {"id":"bd-2hz.3","title":"Impl: Interactive extension UI UX parity (confirm/select/input/editor/notify)","description":"# Goal
Ensure extension UI requests are fully supported in **interactive mode** with great UX and deterministic semantics.

# Scope
- Implement (or audit + complete) interactive handling for:
  - `confirm`
  - `select`
  - `input`
  - `editor`
  - `notify` (fire-and-forget)
- Queue + concurrency:
  - exactly one active UI request at a time
  - bounded queue with clear drop/timeout behavior
  - no deadlocks on shutdown/cancel

# UX Requirements
- Prompts must include provenance:
  - extension id/name
  - requested method
  - timeout (if any)
- Cancellation:
  - user can cancel (Esc/Ctrl+C) and the extension gets a deterministic cancelled response
- Defaults:
  - respect default values per bd-2hz.1

# Logging
- Emit structured logs for UI prompts + responses:
  - request id, method, duration
  - cancelled flag
  - redaction summary

# Tests
- Unit tests for formatting/normalization helpers (where feasible).
- Integration/E2E via tmux capture:
  - run a sample extension that triggers each method
  - capture deterministic artifacts per bd-4u9

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All methods are supported with correct defaults.
- [ ] Cancellation and timeout are deterministic and never deadlock.
- [ ] Interactive UI is clear and attributes requests to the requesting extension.
- [ ] Logs + artifacts are produced and deterministic.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover normalization/formatting helpers and edge cases where applicable
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises interactive UI end-to-end and emits JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include ids, method, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:37:33.838488014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:37:49.236200958Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","extensions","implementation","interactive","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hz.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2hz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:37:33.838488014Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2hz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:37:33.838488014Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2hz.4","title":"Hostcall ABI: UI method routing uses shared dispatcher + taxonomy","description":"# Goal
Ensure extension UI operations are routed through the **shared Hostcall ABI dispatcher** so UI behavior is:
- taxonomy-correct
- logged identically across runtimes
- capability/policy compatible

# Scope
- Implement/finish dispatcher support for `HostCallPayload.method == "ui"` with `params.op` (or equivalent contract per bd-2hz.1).
- Route to the manager/UI surface (interactive + RPC) via a single abstraction.

# Non-Negotiables
- Error taxonomy: `timeout|denied|io|invalid_request|internal` only.
- Logs:
  - `host_call.start/end` must include `params_hash`, `duration_ms`, and `call_id`.
  - Never log raw UI payload fields.
- Determinism:
  - UI request scheduling must not cause synchronous re-entrancy into the JS runtime.

# Tests
- Unit tests:
  - ui.confirm success path
  - UI not configured -> deterministic taxonomy error
  - timeout -> taxonomy timeout
  - cancelled -> deterministic cancelled mapping
  - invalid op/payload -> invalid_request
- Ensure bd-1uy.1.4 parity tests include at least one `ui` case.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shared dispatcher supports `ui.*` operations.
- [ ] UI hostcall mapping produces taxonomy-only error codes.
- [ ] Structured logs are emitted with `params_hash` and no raw payload fields.
- [ ] Unit tests cover success + edge cases.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover ui.* routing, missing handler, timeout, cancel, invalid_request
[ ] Cross-runtime parity coverage exists (bd-1uy.1.4 includes ui cases)
[ ] Logs include ids, params_hash, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:38:15.477084659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:38:29.002020215Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","hostcall","taxonomy","testing","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hz.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:38:15.477084659Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2hz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:38:15.477084659Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2hz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:38:15.477084659Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-m9rk","title":"No-mock: extensions message/session tests use real Session","description":"# Goal
Remove in-memory/session mocks from extension message/session tests and use real `Session` + `SessionHandle` paths.

# Background
`tests/extensions_message_session.rs` defines an in-memory session mock and `tests/e2e_message_session_control.rs` uses `load_extension_with_mocks`. This conflicts with the no-mock policy for core paths; we should exercise the real session plumbing and serialization behavior instead.

# Scope
- Replace in-memory session mock with real `Session` + `SessionHandle` backed by a temp directory.
- Use real extension fixtures (from `tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/**`) instead of mock extension strings where feasible.
- Validate session mutation APIs: append message, set label/name, add custom entry, get branch entries.
- Emit JSONL logs + artifacts (session JSONL, fixture id, event sequence).

# Files
- `tests/extensions_message_session.rs`
- `tests/e2e_message_session_control.rs`
- `tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/**` (fixtures)

# Acceptance
- Tests run against real session persistence (JSONL) and real extension artifacts.
- No in-memory mock session types remain in these tests, unless explicitly allowlisted with rationale in `docs/TEST_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:26:46.911824942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:34:14.146134401Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:34:14.146002815Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m9rk","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:27:00.297279509Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-nom.1","title":"Hostcall ABI: WASM host.call Uses Shared Dispatcher (Parity Refactor)","description":"# Goal
Refactor the WASM component hostcall path (`host.call(name, input_json)` in `src/extensions.rs` `mod wasm_host`) so it uses the shared hostcall dispatcher:

`HostCallPayload (from JSON) -> dispatch_host_call_shared() -> HostResultPayload -> WIT return string`

# Current State (Observed)
- WASM hostcalls are implemented in `src/extensions.rs` under `mod wasm_host`, in `impl host::Host for HostState { async fn call(...) }`.
- The current implementation:
  - parses JSON into `HostCallPayload`
  - validates capability mismatch
  - performs policy + logging + timeout
  - dispatches by method with bespoke logic
- A shared dispatcher already exists (`dispatch_host_call_shared()`), but WASM hostcalls do not yet call it.

# Scope
1. Keep input validation that is specific to the WIT contract:
   - `name` must match `payload.method` (or explicitly document any tolerated mismatch).
   - JSON parse errors are `invalid_request`.

2. Replace the bespoke WASM dispatch path with:
   - Build `HostCallContext`:
     - `runtime = "wasm"`
     - `extension_id` (from registration state)
     - `policy`, `tools`, `http`, `fs`, `env_allowlist`, `manager`
   - Call `dispatch_host_call_shared()`.

3. Convert `HostResultPayload` to the WIT return type:
   - On success: return `Ok(<serialized output JSON>)`.
   - On error: return `Err(<serialized HostCallError JSON>)`.
   - Ensure error codes are taxonomy-only (shared dispatcher guarantees this).

4. Ensure logging is not duplicated:
   - `dispatch_host_call_shared()` already emits `host_call.start/end` and `policy.decision`.
   - Remove duplicate logging from the WASM entrypoint so we don’t double-count or drift.

# Tests
- `--features wasm-host` unit/integration tests:
  - capability mismatch -> `invalid_request`
  - denied -> `denied`
  - timeout -> `timeout` with retryable
  - session/ui/events with manager absent -> deterministic taxonomy
  - params_hash parity vs JS for canonical calls (tie into `bd-1uy.1.4`)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No duplicated policy/timeout/logging logic remains in the WASM hostcall entrypoint.
- [ ] WASM hostcall errors and success outputs match the shared dispatcher’s semantics.
- [ ] `cargo test --all-targets --features wasm-host` is green.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:57:36.997165900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:37:01.317292282Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","hostcall","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nom.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:57:36.997165900Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-nom.1","depends_on_id":"bd-nom","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:57:36.997165900Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-np43","title":"Task: Integrate extension commands into interactive processor","description":"# Task: Integrate Extension Commands into Interactive Processor

## Objective

Modify the interactive command processor to recognize and route extension-registered slash commands.

## Background

The interactive module processes user input, including slash commands. Extension commands must be integrated:
1. When user types /foo, check if foo is an extension command
2. If so, route to extension handler instead of built-in processing
3. Handle the result and display output

## Implementation

### Add Extension Command Check

```rust
async fn process_command(&mut self, input: &str) -> Result<CommandAction> {
    let (cmd, args) = parse_command(input);
    
    // Check built-in commands first (they take precedence)
    match cmd {
        "help" => return self.show_help(),
        "clear" => return self.clear_history(),
        "model" => return self.change_model(args),
        _ => {}
    }
    
    // Check extension commands
    if let Some(runtime) = &self.extension_runtime {
        let ext_commands = runtime.get_registered_commands().await?;
        
        let is_ext_cmd = ext_commands.iter().any(|c| {
            c.name == cmd || c.aliases.contains(&cmd.to_string())
        });
        
        if is_ext_cmd {
            return self.execute_extension_command(cmd, args).await;
        }
    }
    
    Err(anyhow!("Unknown command: /{}", cmd))
}
```

### Execute Extension Command

```rust
async fn execute_extension_command(&mut self, name: &str, args: &str) -> Result<CommandAction> {
    let runtime = self.extension_runtime.as_ref()
        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("No extension runtime"))?;
    
    let result = runtime.execute_command(name, args).await?;
    
    // Display output if any
    if let Some(output) = result.output {
        self.display_output(&output)?;
    }
    
    // Append messages if any
    if let Some(messages) = result.messages {
        for msg in messages {
            self.conversation.push(msg);
        }
    }
    
    if result.continue_loop { Ok(CommandAction::Continue) } else { Ok(CommandAction::Exit) }
}
```

### Update Help Command

```rust
async fn show_help(&self) -> Result<CommandAction> {
    println!("Built-in commands:");
    println!("  /help    - Show this help");
    println!("  /clear   - Clear conversation");
    
    if let Some(runtime) = &self.extension_runtime {
        let ext_commands = runtime.get_registered_commands().await?;
        
        if !ext_commands.is_empty() {
            println!("\nExtension commands:");
            for cmd in ext_commands {
                let usage = cmd.usage.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
                println!("  /{:<8} - {} {}", cmd.name, cmd.description, usage);
            }
        }
    }
    
    Ok(CommandAction::Continue)
}
```

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (7 cases)
1. test_builtin_commands_still_work
2. test_extension_command_recognized
3. test_extension_command_executed_via_js
4. test_output_displayed_correctly
5. test_unknown_command_returns_error
6. test_builtin_commands_take_precedence
7. test_extension_aliases_work

### E2E Test Script (tests/e2e/interactive_commands.sh)

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

LOG_FILE="artifacts/interactive_commands_e2e.jsonl"
mkdir -p artifacts

log() {
    echo "{\"timestamp\":\"$(date -Iseconds)\",\"event\":\"$1\",\"data\":$2}" >> "$LOG_FILE"
}

log "test_start" '{"suite":"interactive_commands"}'

# Create test extension
mkdir -p /tmp/test_interactive_ext
cat > /tmp/test_interactive_ext/index.js << 'EOF'
export function activate(ctx) {
    ctx.registerCommand({
        name: "echo",
        description: "Echo arguments back",
        aliases: ["e"],
        handler: async (args) => ({ output: args || "(empty)" }),
    });
}
EOF

# Test with tmux for interactive mode
if command -v tmux &> /dev/null; then
    SESSION="pi_test_$$"
    tmux new-session -d -s "$SESSION" "pi --extension /tmp/test_interactive_ext"
    sleep 2
    
    # Type /help and capture
    tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" "/help" Enter
    sleep 1
    HELP_OUTPUT=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$SESSION" -p)
    log "help_output" "{\"contains_echo\":$(echo "$HELP_OUTPUT" | grep -q "echo" && echo true || echo false)}"
    
    # Type /echo test
    tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" "/echo hello world" Enter
    sleep 1
    ECHO_OUTPUT=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$SESSION" -p)
    log "echo_output" "{\"output\":\"$ECHO_OUTPUT\"}"
    
    # Type /e (alias)
    tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" "/e alias test" Enter
    sleep 1
    
    # Cleanup
    tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" "/quit" Enter
    sleep 1
    tmux kill-session -t "$SESSION" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    log "test_end" '{"status":"pass"}'
else
    log "test_skip" '{"reason":"tmux not available"}'
fi

echo "Interactive commands E2E test complete. Logs: $LOG_FILE"
```

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-ie1g (execute_command)
- Depends on: bd-2qrn (get_registered_commands)
- Part of: bd-20vx (Slash Command Handler feature)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Extension commands recognized by processor
- [ ] Built-in commands take precedence
- [ ] Extension commands appear in /help
- [ ] Results displayed correctly
- [ ] Aliases work
- [ ] 7 unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E test passes with JSONL logging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:03:18.904597311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:21:46.301657257Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:21:46.301593358Z","close_reason":"All implemented: list_commands() queries registered commands from JS snapshot. execute_command() at extensions.rs:4685 routes to __pi_execute_command in JS. dispatch_extension_command() in interactive.rs:5578 integrates with interactive processor.  {"id":"bd-ocv","title":"Unit tests: error type conversions + display invariants","description":"# Goal
Cover `src/error.rs` with unit tests for constructors, From conversions, and user-facing Display messages.

# Scope / Deliverables
- `Error::config/session/provider/auth/tool/validation/extension/api` constructors.
- `From<std::io::Error>`, `From<serde_json::Error>`, `From<sqlmodel_core::Error>`, `From<asupersync::sync::LockError>` mappings.
- Display formatting includes context (provider/tool names, paths).
- Ensure errors are stable for snapshot/log comparisons.

# No‑Mock Rule
- Use real error types and real error values (temp files, malformed JSON, sqlite errors).

# Acceptance
- 12+ unit tests covering all variants and conversions.
- Display strings include expected context and are non-empty.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:26:04.321594937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:25:19.168939132Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:54.716952566Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ocv","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-ocv","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":113,"issue_id":"bd-ocv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Notes:
- Include a test that wraps an io::Error from temp file access and asserts the Display prefix `IO error:`.
- Cover LockError::Cancelled -> Error::Aborted mapping and LockError::Poisoned -> session error.
- Ensure provider/tool errors include provider/tool names in Display strings.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:27:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ofcj","title":"Expand proven Pi extension coverage (research→conformance)","description":"# Goal
Dramatically expand the set of Pi extensions that are **proven to work unmodified** across all major extension shapes (skills, prompts, tools, MCP servers, providers, templates, packages).

# Outcome
A curated, version‑pinned catalog with conformance + performance gates that prove compatibility, reliability, and speed.

# Scope
Research → selection → acquisition → conformance → performance → documentation → refresh workflow.

# Non‑Goals
Do not modify third‑party extension source code; we only vendor exact upstream artifacts and validate them.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:18:34.843067957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:18:30.519275035Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":317,"issue_id":"bd-ofcj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: The Pi extension ecosystem is spread across npm, GitHub, and template/skill packs, and current conformance coverage only proves a limited subset.

Reasoning: Proving that popular extensions run unmodified reduces maintenance and increases user trust; this requires a full pipeline from research to testing to publication.

Considerations: Artifacts must remain byte-for-byte upstream, version-pinned, and validated with correctness + performance + reliability gates.

Exit criteria: A curated catalog and test reports exist, all selected extensions pass or are explicitly excluded with rationale.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:44:52Z"},{"id":386,"issue_id":"bd-ofcj","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"SCALE TARGET (USER REQUIREMENT)

Given the ecosystem size (OpenClaw/marketplaces + GitHub/npm), the “proven unmodified” set must be large enough to be convincing.

Target
- Tier-1 corpus: >= 200 extensions (true Pi extensions, unmodified)
- Tier-0 baseline: official pi-mono examples remain must-pass

Evidence requirements
- Deterministic conformance + perf/reliability gates with JSONL logs + diff artifacts.
- Offline/fixture mode in CI; live refresh is opt-in.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:18:30Z"}]}
